document.source_title: Palestine: Killings, destruction and settler encroachment continues
document.summary: The UN report details extensive violations of international law in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, highlighting war crimes committed by both Israeli and Palestinian parties. Despite a ceasefire in October 2025, violence and humanitarian crises persist, with significant casualties and displacement. The report emphasizes the urgent need for accountability and action to address the ongoing violations and humanitarian needs of Palestinians.
key_takeaways: UN report covers violations from October 2023 to May 2025.
Israeli forces killed 72,769 Palestinians since October 2023.
Palestinian armed groups committed war crimes against Israeli civilians.
Settlement expansion in the West Bank increased by 80% since the current government took office.
33,000 Palestinians displaced from refugee camps remain unable to return home.
Impunity for violations continues to fuel further violence.
document.why_it_matters: The report underscores the critical humanitarian situation in the Occupied Palestinian Territory and the need for international accountability and intervention to protect human rights and prevent further atrocities.

claims.claim_text: Israeli forces have killed 72,769 Palestinians since October 2023.
claims.evidence_text: The Israeli military has killed 72,769 Palestinians since 7 October 2023 in Gaza.

claims.claim_text: Palestinian armed groups committed war crimes against Israeli civilians.
claims.evidence_text: Palestinian armed groups committed war crimes and possible crimes against humanity when they attacked civilians in Israel on and after 7 October 2023.

claims.claim_text: Settlement expansion in the West Bank has increased by 80%.
claims.evidence_text: The dispossession in the West Bank is matched in intensity with the record rate of settlement expansion which has increased by 80 percent since the Government took office.

entities.name: UN Human Rights
entities.description: The UN body responsible for promoting and protecting human rights globally.
entities.evidence_text: Ajith Sunghay, Head of UN Human Rights in the Occupied Palestinian Territory (OPT), launched its latest report.

entities.name: Hamas
entities.description: A Palestinian militant organization involved in the conflict with Israel.
entities.evidence_text: Hamas continues its own violations, including against the people of Gaza.

entities.name: Israeli Government
entities.description: The governing body of Israel, involved in the ongoing conflict and settlement expansion.
entities.evidence_text: The Israeli Government has intensified the militarisation of the settler movement.

events.evidence_text: The reporting period saw unprecedented levels of killings of Palestinians by Israeli forces, the tightening and escalation of Israeli control over Palestinians and their land.
The ceasefire diminished the immense scale of violence up that point and opened some modest humanitarian space.

citations.title: Palestine: Killings, destruction and settler encroachment continues

source_credibility.reason: The report is published by the UN Human Rights office.
